clinical_criteria: Within 365 days before through 365 days after a qualifying CRC diagnosis code
Presence of surgical procedure codes to treat CRC within the specified timeframe
Presence of chemotherapy or radiation therapy procedure codes within the specified timeframe
No evidence of any other cancer diagnosis (for chemotherapy/radiation therapy path)
Simple string searches applied to pathology reports
algorithm_summary: This colorectal cancer phenotype uses a hierarchical 3-path approach: 1) First, identify cases through cancer registry data (highest priority), 2) Second, identify cases with CRC diagnosis codes plus surgical procedures within 365 days, 3) Third, identify cases with CRC diagnosis codes plus chemotherapy/radiation therapy within 365 days (excluding those with other cancers). All subjects are first screened to exclude those with ulcerative enterocolitis or Crohn's disease using specific ICD codes. The algorithm also defines two types of controls - those with and without evidence of CRC screening. The phenotype relies exclusively on structured data and simple pathology report string searches, not complex NLP.
